summary: Two-pass rescale on an i64 slice composing two tail-recursive helpers (min_at computing the suffix-minimum, shift_at subtracting delta into a Vec via extend_from_slice with a typed [i64;1] chunk); each helper proved by strong induction on size − i.toNat, then composed in a rescale_to_unit_aux lemma — min_at_correct produces both a lower-bound (m ≤ numbers[j]) AND an extremum-witness existential (m = numbers[j*] for some j*), the witness is fed into hfit on the pair (j, j*) to discharge shift_at's pointwise no-subtraction-overflow hypothesis (via Int64.subOverflow_iff), and three top-level theorems (preserves_length / output_min_is_zero / is_uniform_shift) each specialize the aux lemma, with a separate short_input_returns_empty boundary theorem for len<2.
